mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or the out-of-sample Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, it signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces consumer spending capacity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power with a Rank IC below 0.03 during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and consumer prices indicate strong demand outpacing supply, which forces corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction term's marginal predictive contribution is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production signals expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week specifically during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery periods, with a hit rate below 50% or a negative Rank IC over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding aggregate demand and corporate profit foundations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth correlates negatively with 60-day forward returns, or the signal's out-of-sample R-squared falls below 0.01 across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly inflation accelerates beyond 0.4%, it triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of high-inflation months, or the threshold effect vanishes when controlling for prior rate expectations in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which boosts wage growth and consumer confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative forward returns in >55% of observations, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over single-series models in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts signals weakening residential investment and broader demand contraction, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: Housing starts declines fail to predict negative 60-day returns during late-cycle phases, with a hit rate below 45% or a statistically insignificant predictive coefficient (p > 0.05).
